description_updates.ai_description: Philippe L. Bédard's research focuses on cancer genomics, diagnostics, immunotherapy, and biomarkers, with specific interests in lung cancer treatments, mutations, and advanced breast cancer therapies, particularly involving HER2/EGFR [1]. He has authored several papers on topics including Tucatinib for HER2-positive breast cancer with brain metastases, a phase I trial of CX-5461, the impact of antibiotics on immune checkpoint inhibitor treatment, and the anti-CEACAM5 antibody-drug conjugate tusamitamab ravtansine [2, 3, 4, 5]. Bédard has also co-authored research on leveraging big data for immune checkpoint blockade response and has collaborated with researchers such as Aaron R. Hansen, Benjamin Haibe-Kains, and Geoffrey Liu [6, 7, 8, 9].
